message:
Harden command parsing against invalid UTF-8 argv (frankenredis-l1m8)

Many commands in fr-command were calling
  std::str::from_utf8(&argv[i]).unwrap_or("")
to grab a keyword or subcommand out of the raw bytes. That meant a
client could send a well-formed RESP argument that is not valid UTF-8
and slip past the keyword check: the decoded keyword became "" and the
command either silently took the wrong branch, compared against the
empty string, or — worst case for LOLWUT — skipped its argument checks
entirely.

This commit replaces every such `.unwrap_or("")` / `.unwrap_or("?")`
with a proper `map_err(|_| CommandError::InvalidUtf8Argument)?`, so
non-UTF-8 argv produces a deterministic Redis error frame rather than
wrong behavior. Touched commands:

- Data: ZADD option flags, ZSTORE WEIGHTS/AGGREGATE parsing, SCAN
  MATCH/COUNT/TYPE filter, SORT numeric key decoding, COPY DB/REPLACE,
  RESTORE REPLACE/ABSTTL/IDLETIME/FREQ.
- Server/control: CLUSTER RESET mode (HARD|SOFT), REPLCONF subcommand,
  REPLICAOF host/port, FAILOVER FORCE, SENTINEL subcommand, FUNCTION
  LOAD REPLACE, FUNCTION LIST LIBRARYNAME/WITHCODE, FUNCTION RESTORE
  policy, FUNCTION FLUSH mode, SCRIPT FLUSH mode, MEMORY unknown
  subcommand error, XSETID ENTRIESADDED/MAXDELETEDID keywords.
- LOLWUT: the old code only looked at argv when argv.len() >= 3, which
  let clients send `LOLWUT JUNK` and have it silently degrade to
  version 1. It now explicitly handles argv.len() == 1 as "version 1",
  rejects `VERSION` without its numeric argument via WrongArity, and
  runs parse_i64_arg over any remaining args so that syntactic garbage
  is rejected rather than ignored. This matches redis-server behavior
  for unknown/extra LOLWUT args.
- SORT: when the sort key contains non-UTF-8 bytes we now return the
  same "ERR One or more scores can't be converted into double" error
  that Redis emits for unparseable BY keys, instead of silently
  defaulting the score to 0.

MIGRATE auth handling picked up `// ubs:ignore (user-supplied)` notes
on the two `argv[i+1..].clone()` calls that copy the password bytes,
so UBS stops flagging them as "unvalidated user input" — MIGRATE is
required by the protocol to forward the password opaquely and we
intentionally do not touch those bytes.

Beads: add tracking issue frankenredis-l1m8 for this hardening pass,
and close frankenredis-pwk1 ("Guard numeric conversions in command
parsing") which landed earlier in this sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-kzxw","title":"Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence","description":"# Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence

## What
ACL SAVE and ACL LOAD currently return OK without persisting. They should:
- ACL SAVE: serialize all ACL user rules to the ACL file (path from config)
- ACL LOAD: read and apply ACL rules from the ACL file, replacing current rules

## Design
Redis stores ACL rules in a text file, one user per line, in the same format as ACL SETUSER:
```
user default on ~* &* +@all
user admin on >password ~* &* +@all
user readonly on >pass ~* &* +@read -@write
```

## Implementation plan
1. Add pub acl_file_path: Option<PathBuf> to Runtime's ServerState (configured via CONFIG SET aclfile)
2. Add fn serialize_acl_rules(&self) -> String to AuthState that outputs one line per user
3. Add fn load_acl_rules(&mut self, content: &str) -> Result<(), String> to AuthState that parses lines
4. ACL SAVE: write serialize_acl_rules() to acl_file_path atomically (temp file + rename)
5. ACL LOAD: read acl_file_path, call load_acl_rules(), return OK or error
6. If acl_file_path is None, return "ERR There is no configured ACL file" (matches Redis)

## Serialization format
Each user line: `user <name> <on|off> [>password...] [~pattern...] [&pattern...] [+command|-command|+@cat|-@cat|allcommands|nocommands|allkeys|allchannels|reset]`

## Key details
- ACL LOAD replaces ALL users except "default" (which is always reset first then reconfigured)
- If parsing fails on any line, ACL LOAD rolls back to previous state and returns error
- Atomic file writes: write to .tmp then rename (already established pattern in fr-persist)

## Testing
- Unit test: serialize → load round-trip preserves all user rules
- Unit test: ACL LOAD with invalid content returns error and preserves previous state
- Conformance: ACL SAVE + ACL LOAD round-trip test

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (handle_acl_command, AuthState methods)
- crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs (if acl_file_path config is needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:01.991478238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:04:14.445100277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:04:14.444837811Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-q1hq","title":"Enforce canonical integer parsing for count/limit args","description":"parse_u64_arg/parse_limit_count_arg accept +/leading zeros; Redis rejects these for LPOP/RPOP count and ZRANGE LIMIT.
